title: COPPA §312.3 — Verifiable parental consent: consent-record completeness and audit trail
exclusions: Does NOT substitute for the full regulatory control set; this UC provides one of several evidence streams and must be combined with policy, procedure, and supplemental UCs to demonstrate the complete clause.
evidence: Scheduled search output persisted to the compliance summary index with regulation=COPPA, clause=§312.3, available as a saved search and exportable as a CSV evidence pack for the responsible officer.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Phase-C authored UC targeting COPPA §312.3 (Verifiable parental consent obligations). Monitors consent-record completeness for child accounts; assurance level set to 'contributing' pending SME review per Phase E of the regulation-coverage plan.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that COPPA §312.3 (Verifiable parental consent obligations) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.50.29: COPPA §312.3 — Verifiable parental consent: consent-record completeness and audit trail.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.50.29' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.
compliance.smeCaveat: Phase-C draft: SME review required before uplifting assurance from 'contributing' to 'partial' or 'full'. Confirm that the SPL captures the specific regulator expectation and that the telemetry exists in the deployment.

dataSources: User registration logs, consent-management platform (CMP) events, age-gate verification records.
spl:
index=user_mgmt sourcetype IN (registration, consent_records) earliest=-30d
| search user_age_bracket="under_13" OR account_type="child"
| eval has_parental_consent=if(isnotnull(parental_consent_date) AND parental_consent_method IN ("signed_form", "credit_card_verification", "government_id", "video_call", "knowledge_based"), 1, 0)
| eval consent_valid=if(has_parental_consent=1 AND parental_consent_date<=_time, 1, 0)
| stats count AS child_accounts sum(consent_valid) AS with_valid_consent dc(eval(if(consent_valid=0, user_id, null()))) AS missing_consent_users BY service, registration_source
| eval consent_coverage_pct=round(100*with_valid_consent/child_accounts, 1)
| where consent_coverage_pct<100
| table service, registration_source, child_accounts, with_valid_consent, missing_consent_users, consent_coverage_pct
description: Monitors child-account registrations and verifies that verifiable parental consent records exist per §312.3 requirements, flagging accounts lacking valid consent documentation.
value: §312.3 requires operators to obtain verifiable parental consent before collecting personal information from children under 13. Continuous monitoring ensures no child account operates without documented consent.
implementation: (1) Ingest registration and consent-management logs with user_age_bracket, parental_consent_date, parental_consent_method; (2) schedule daily; (3) immediately flag accounts without consent for remediation.
visualization: Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner.
knownFalsePositives: Tuning prerequisites: source field mappings must be in place before the UC will produce reliable metrics; early runs will commonly flag unmapped systems until onboarding is completed.
grandmaExplanation: COPPA §312.3 requires parental consent before collecting children's data — so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: User registration logs, consent-management platform (CMP) events, age-gate verification records.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Ingest registration and consent-management logs with user_age_bracket, parental_consent_date, parental_consent_method; (2) schedule daily; (3) immediately flag accounts without consent for remediation.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=user_mgmt sourcetype IN (registration, consent_records) earliest=-30d
| search user_age_bracket="under_13" OR account_type="child"
| eval has_parental_consent=if(isnotnull(parental_consent_date) AND parental_consent_method IN ("signed_form", "credit_card_verification", "government_id", "video_call", "knowledge_based"), 1, 0)
| eval consent_valid=if(has_parental_consent=1 AND parental_consent_date<=_time, 1, 0)
| stats count AS child_accounts sum(consent_valid) AS with_valid_consent dc(eval(if(consent_valid=0, user_id, null()))) AS missing_consent_users BY service, registration_source
| eval consent_coverage_pct=round(100*with_valid_consent/child_accounts, 1)
| where consent_coverage_pct<100
| table service, registration_source, child_accounts, with_valid_consent, missing_consent_users, consent_coverage_pct
```

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner.
